athlete_safe_copy: Genetics can help frame how an athlete may respond to power, endurance, and mixed training exposures.
practitioner_copy: Use as a contextual planning input for training emphasis, response monitoring, and coaching communication.
caution_copy: This is probabilistic context only. It should not be used to identify talent, limit opportunity, or predict ceiling.

athlete_safe_copy: DNA can help personalize how fueling and micronutrient support are discussed.
practitioner_copy: Useful for tailoring carbohydrate emphasis, vitamin support conversations, and practical recovery nutrition habits.
caution_copy: Do not present this domain as destiny. Nutrition planning must still be driven by behavior, labs, and goals.

athlete_safe_copy: This domain helps explain why some athletes may need more deliberate recovery support.
practitioner_copy: Use alongside blood markers, wellness trends, and training density rather than as a standalone risk label.
caution_copy: No genetic panel should be framed as a guaranteed injury or recovery outcome.

athlete_safe_copy: This domain can guide sleep coaching and consistency habits.
practitioner_copy: Helpful for bedtime routines, travel preparation, and coaching language around recovery priorities.
caution_copy: Sleep behavior and environment still matter more than genotype alone.

athlete_safe_copy: This domain can support conversations about attention, learning, and response style.
practitioner_copy: Use to tailor cueing, drill progression, and learning environments without making fixed behavioral claims.
caution_copy: Never use this as a selection tool or as proof of cognitive limitations.
